bulletins.highlights: The avalanches can occur several times in the same paths.

avalancheActivity.highlights: As a consequence of new snow and warming a precarious avalanche situation will persist. New snow and wind slabs represent the main danger.
avalancheActivity.comment:
As a consequence of new snow and a strong southwesterly wind, avalanche prone wind slabs formed. They can be released easily and reach very large size in isolated cases. This applies in particular on steep slopes above the tree line, as well as in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain. The avalanche prone locations are sometimes covered with new snow and are difficult to recognise.

In many cases avalanches can be triggered in the various wind slab layers. Already many medium-sized and, in many cases, large natural dry avalanches are possible as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation.

Whumpfing sounds and the formation of shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack and natural avalanches are a clear indication of a weakly bonded snowpack. Backcountry touring calls for experience in the assessment of avalanche danger and careful route selection.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Above approximately 1000 m there is still a very large amount of snow. Over a wide area 30 to 50 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, has fallen since Tuesday above approximately 1500 m. As a consequence of the strong westerly wind, fresh snow drift accumulations formed.
Over a wide area new snow and wind slabs are lying on a weakly bonded old snowpack.

tendency.highlights: Above approximately 700 m snow will fall until late morning over a wide area. Slight increase in avalanche danger as a consequence of new snow and wind.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Considerable avalanche danger will prevail. The new snow and wind slabs represent the main danger.
avalancheActivity.comment:
As a consequence of new snow and a moderate wind, sometimes large wind slabs formed. Wind slabs can be released, even by a single winter sport participant. This applies in particular on steep slopes, as well as in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain also at intermediate altitudes. Some small and medium-sized natural dry avalanches are possible as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation.
The avalanche prone locations are sometimes covered with new snow difficult to recognise. Whumpfing sounds and the formation of shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack and natural avalanches are a clear indication of a weakly bonded snowpack.
Avalanches can be released in deeper layers in particular on steep shady slopes.
Careful route selection and spacing between individuals are recommended.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Over a wide area 20 to 25 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, has fallen since Tuesday. As a consequence of new snow and a moderate to strong wind from southerly directions, precarious wind slabs formed in particular above the tree line. The new snow and wind slabs are lying on soft layers in particular on wind-protected shady slopes.

Intermediate and high altitudes: Isolated avalanche prone weak layers exist in the bottom section of the snowpack in particular on very steep shady slopes.

tendency.highlights: Above approximately 1000 m snow will fall until late morning in some regions. Slight increase in avalanche danger as a consequence of new snow and wind.

avalancheActivity.highlights: At intermediate and high altitudes a considerable avalanche danger will prevail.
avalancheActivity.comment:
As a consequence of new snow and a sometimes strong wind from westerly directions, avalanche prone wind slabs formed. This applies in particular on steep slopes, as well as in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain also at intermediate altitudes. The fresh wind slabs can be released by small loads. The avalanche prone locations are sometimes covered with new snow and are barely recognisable.

Some small and medium-sized natural dry avalanches are possible as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation.

Avalanches can be released in deeper layers in particular on steep shady slopes. Several medium-sized and, in isolated cases, large natural dry avalanches are possible as a consequence of new snow and strong wind.

Maintaining distances between individuals and one-at-a-time descents are recommended.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Up to 15 cm of snow has fallen since Tuesday above approximately 1500 m. As a consequence of new snow and a moderate wind, further wind slabs formed on Tuesday. The new snow and wind slabs are lying on soft layers in particular on wind-protected shady slopes.

Intermediate and high altitudes: Isolated avalanche prone weak layers exist in the bottom section of the snowpack in particular on very steep shady slopes.

tendency.highlights: Above approximately 1000 m snow will fall until late morning in some regions. Slight increase in avalanche danger as a consequence of new snow and wind.
